name: Heirloom Raisin Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine raisins and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover.
2. Simmer for 20 minutes. Drain the raisins, reserving the liquid.
3. Add enough of the reserved liquid to make 1/2 cup. Cool well.
4. Cream together butter and sugar in a bowl until light and fluffy using an electric mixer at medium speed.
5. Add eggs, beating for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Sift together flour and baking powder.
7. Alternately add the flour mixture with 1/2 cup of the reserved raisin liquid into the creamed mixture, mixing well after each addition.
8. Stir in the raisins.
9. Spoon batter into greased muffin cups, filling them about 2/3 full.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F for 18 minutes or until golden brown. Serve hot with homemade jam or jelly.
